trevor-bachmeyer.acute_vs_chronic_note: Dr Bachmeyer often distinguishes acute (injury/infection) vs chronic (longevity/maintenance) dosing. Acute_signals + chronic_signals fields surface candidate contexts; full distillation pending Codex pass.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider GHRP-6 only as a niche short-acting appetite-stimulant for the rare patient who genuinely struggles to eat — Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:42.720): "the use case for these would be if you have someone that is very hesitant that is struggling to eat and if they're struggling to put on weight and they're struggling to eat, these can be beneficial if you just take them right before a meal because they're so short acting." For all other GH-secretagogue indications we'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh the cleaner ipamorelin (± CJC-1295 no-DAC) alternative because all three experts converge on GHRP-6 as 'messy/junk/dirtier' relative to ipamorelin — Trevor (WIHm62bBsUI 00:15:11.120): "the other dirtier secrets like GHRP6 for example. That's junk."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Ravenous appetite + dose-window: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:10.520): "GHRP-6 can cause ravenous, near uncontrollable hunger within 15 to 20 minutes of injection, uh, which is obviously counterproductive to us that are trying to maintain body composition."
- Prolactin + cortisol elevation: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:20.440): "and also significantly elevates prolactin and cortisol"; Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "GHRP-6 and MK will make you hungry enough to eat a buffet, and GHRP-2 can raise cortisol"; Taylor (5ugqlYRSRSo 01:12:10.719): "too, you have to be careful with those, the prolactin and cortisol."
- Ghrelin-receptor desensitization with chronic use: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:29.360): "and both ghrelin receptor desensitization with chronic use"; Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:31.560): "uh, requires dose escalation over time, which is again a fundamental design flaw in those."
- Body-composition / fat-loss interference: Hunter email threadId 19d5563af433c548 (2026-04-03): "Why Ipa won't mess with fat loss goals the way **MK-677 or GHRP-6** might."
- Do not stack with other GHRPs: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "And you don't combine multiple GHRPs either (like Ipamorelin + MK-677 + GHRP-6). That's too much on the ghrelin side."
- No 2023 baseline_protocol exists for GHRP-6 in Hunter's cheat sheet — Taylor (5ugqlYRSRSo 01:11:30.560): "Couldn't find any info from Hunter on GHRP2, GHRP6 on peptide cheat sheet in videos. No, I just haven't really talked about those" — every recommendation here is 2025-2026 transcript content only.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: All three experts agree GHRP-6 is functionally inferior to ipamorelin for general GH-secretagogue use because the appetite/prolactin/cortisol side-effect profile is meaningfully dirtier. Mechanism + class consensus — Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:06:46.200): "my alternatives to AOD. The next one is going to be GHRP-2 and GHRP-6. Uh, basically these were early synthetic GH secretagogues from the ghrelin mimetics"; Taylor (JccJTHbWF84 00:17:36.799): "The issue with MK677 and really GHRP2 and GHRP6 is when you do this with via the ghrelin agonism, it spikes your appetite out of control"; Trevor (WIHm62bBsUI 00:15:05.839): "a very clean, very sharp signal for growth hormone release without all the side effect profile of the other dirtier secrets like GHRP6 for example. That's junk." Better-alternative consensus — Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:08:14.040): "We have ipamorelin, which has all the benefits of the GHRP on the pituitary via ghrelin, but we don't get the prolactin and cortisol increase and we don't get the increase in appetite for most people"; Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "For anti-aging and longevity, the best combo is almost always CJC no DAC + Ipamorelin or Tesamorelin + Ipamorelin"; Taylor (5ugqlYRSRSo 01:11:40.080): "why would you use those when we have Tessa and IPA?" Hunger/appetite consensus — Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:10.520): "ravenous, near uncontrollable hunger within 15 to 20 minutes of injection"; Taylor (IW0-bllx4dk 00:23:38.080): "Any peptides that stimulate hunger, appetite? There are GHRP2, GHRP6"; Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "GHRP-6 and MK will make you hungry enough to eat a buffet."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: The experts do NOT meaningfully disagree on GHRP-6 — Hunter, Taylor and Trevor all converge on it being inferior to ipamorelin and recommend against routine use. The only nuance is whether the appetite spike is exploitable: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:42.720) carves out a single niche — "if you have someone that is very hesitant that is struggling to eat and if they're struggling to put on weight and they're struggling to eat, these can be beneficial if you just take them right before a meal because they're so short acting" — and Taylor (5ugqlYRSRSo 01:11:53.440) agrees "If someone needs to eat, meaning if they need an appetite, I would actually use those because they can increase appetite," while Trevor (WIHm62bBsUI 00:15:11.120) categorically writes it off — "That's junk" — without acknowledging the appetite-niche. Hunter and Taylor preserve a narrow underweight/struggle-to-eat use case; Trevor does not. No expert published a defensible mcg dose for GHRP-6 — surface this absence rather than invent one.

acute.dose: No expert publishes a verbatim mcg dose for GHRP-6 acute use. Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:42.720): "the use case for these would be if you have someone that is very hesitant that is struggling to eat and if they're struggling to put on weight and they're struggling to eat, these can be beneficial if you just take them right before a meal because they're so short acting."
acute.frequency: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:48.040): "if you just take them right before a meal because they're so short acting"; Taylor (IW0-bllx4dk 00:23:46.640): "I would just do that in a shorter" (window).
acute.duration: Short-acting / pre-meal only — no chronic frame in acute appetite use.
acute.verbatim_citation: Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:42.720): "the use case for these would be if you have someone that is very hesitant that is struggling to eat and if they're struggling to put on weight and they're struggling to eat, these can be beneficial if you just take them right before a meal because they're so short acting." Taylor (5ugqlYRSRSo 01:11:53.440): "If someone needs to eat, meaning if they need an appetite, I would actually use those because they can increase appetite."

chronic.dose: No expert recommends GHRP-6 chronically. Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:29.360): "and both ghrelin receptor desensitization with chronic use"; Hunter (ODWpYYPTB_8 00:07:31.560): "uh, requires dose escalation over time, which is again a fundamental design flaw in those."
chronic.verbatim_citation: Hunter email threadId 19a7529f70f91a99 (2025-11-11): "For anti-aging and longevity, the best combo is almost always CJC no DAC + Ipamorelin or Tesamorelin + Ipamorelin, depending on how aggressive you want to go." Trevor (WIHm62bBsUI 00:15:11.120): "the other dirtier secrets like GHRP6 for example. That's junk." Taylor (5ugqlYRSRSo 01:11:40.080): "why would you use those when we have Tessa and IPA?"
